About Me

Hello!

Hi, my name is JYOTSHNA KANNURU, a Cloud Engineer with 10+ years of experience supporting cloud infrastructure, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) practices, platform operations, release reliability, and production systems across telecom, SaaS, e-commerce, and enterprise environments. Skilled in building and supporting secure, scalable cloud environments across AWS and Azure, with strong ownership of infrastructure automation, Kubernetes platforms, CI/CD delivery, Linux operations, and incident response.

Cloud & DevOps Focus

Building Secure, Scalable, and Resilient Cloud Systems

Combining Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD automation, and Site Reliability Engineering to deliver robust platform operations across AWS and Azure.

01

Cloud Architecture & IaC

  • Provisioning scalable cloud infrastructure using Terraform, CloudFormation, and Terragrunt across AWS and Azure.
  • Designing secure networking topologies, VPCs, Subnets, and transit gateways for enterprise workloads.
  • Managing cloud access securely using Azure RBAC, AWS IAM, and zero-trust security policies.
  • Optimizing cloud resource consumption and leading cost management initiatives to reduce infrastructure spend.
02

Kubernetes & Container Orchestration

  • Containerizing monolithic applications and deploying them to scalable Kubernetes clusters (EKS & AKS).
  • Orchestrating GitOps-driven application deployments using Argo CD, Helm, and Kubernetes namespaces.
  • Managing cluster networking, Ingress controllers, and high availability for critical application services.
  • Enforcing cluster security with RBAC, secrets management, and policy-as-code validations.
03

CI/CD & Automation

  • Building robust CI/CD pipelines using GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, and GitHub Actions for continuous delivery.
  • Automating complex operational tasks, patching, and deployments with Python, Bash, and Ansible.
  • Integrating security scanning (Trivy, SonarQube, SAST, DAST) directly into deployment workflows.
  • Implementing blue-green and canary release strategies to ensure zero-downtime application updates.
04

Observability & SRE

  • Designing comprehensive observability stacks with Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk, and ELK.
  • Establishing reliable alerting and incident triage workflows to reduce Mean Time to Resolution (MTTR).
  • Building AIOps incident triage systems using GenAI tools to accelerate root-cause analysis.
  • Leading disaster recovery planning, resilience validation, and maintaining strict service level objectives.
Turning ideas into reality!

Career History

Professional Experience

Supporting cloud infrastructure, SRE practices, CI/CD, and production systems across telecom, SaaS, and enterprise environments.

May 2025 - Present

Cloud Engineer

T-Mobile (Client) | CNet Global Solutions | Infosys Limited|Richardson, TX

  • Managed AWS telecom cloud infrastructure with VPC, IAM, EC2, EKS, Route 53, health checks, and failover runbooks, keeping regional recovery within 15 minutes during resilience validation.
  • Built an AIOps incident triage workflow using Azure OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, LangChain, RAG, Kubernetes, Splunk Observability, and Python automation, giving support teams clearer root-cause and remediation guidance.
  • Orchestrated blue-green and canary deployments through GitLab CI/CD, Helm, Argo CD, and Kubernetes, supporting controlled release movement for 300+ cloud-hosted application services.
  • Standardized AWS provisioning with Terraform, Terragrunt, CloudFormation, remote state, and state locking, decreasing infrastructure drift by 30% across production and non-production environments.
  • Configured GitOps delivery for Amazon EKS with Argo CD, Helm, Kubernetes RBAC, namespaces, and approval workflows, giving developers traceable deployments and cleaner release handoffs.
  • Hardened cloud security controls with HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, AWS KMS, AWS ACM, Trivy, SonarQube, SAST, and DAST, cutting vulnerability remediation backlog by 25%.
  • Expanded observability coverage with Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk Observability, CloudWatch, and OpenTelemetry, giving cloud operations teams stronger visibility into logs, metrics, traces, and service health.
  • Scripted cloud operations tasks with Python, Bash, Ansible, and AWS Systems Manager to support patching, certificate validation, deployment checks, and incident diagnostics across Linux, Kubernetes, DNS, and CI/CD workflows.

Agentic AIOps Incident Triage

Built an AIOps triage workflow with Azure OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, LangChain, RAG, Kubernetes, GitLab CI/CD, Grafana, and Splunk Observability to connect deployment changes, pod events, and alert signals into incident summaries. Developed Python and Bash collectors for pod health, container restarts, node status, and ingress failures.

Multi-Cloud Infrastructure Automation
